scenario.name: Solo Baseline
scenario.hypothesis: A localized task with one clear owner should favor the single-agent arm; multi-agent arms should pay coordination overhead without enough upside.
scenario.expectedDivergence: single should have lower duration, fewer spans, fewer tool calls, and comparable artifact quality.
scenario.taskPacket:
# Agent Coordination Patterns Task Packet

This packet is self-contained. Do not inspect the repository, shell, or filesystem.
Use only the Firegrid session and coordination-channel tools exposed to you.

Scenario: Solo Baseline

Provided materials:

Experiment goal:
Firegrid should act as the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: one participant, a central orchestrator, and peer choreography.

Current harness shape:
- The driver composes a real Firegrid host in-process.
- Participants are launched through the public Firegrid client/session surface.
- The shared board is exposed as channels: coordination.work, coordination.claims, coordination.findings, coordination.questions, coordination.reviews, and coordination.final.
- The driver injects inbound events through Firegrid.channels.send and waits for the final artifact through Firegrid.channels.waitFor.
- A run is incomplete unless a coordination.final row is published.

Known measurement risks:
- If the task packet asks for repository or shell access, agents may burn time on unavailable tools instead of exercising Firegrid coordination.
- If final artifacts are optional prose instead of channel rows, the scorer cannot reliably compare arms.
- Choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not by hidden driver state.

Task:
- Identify one small documentation or workflow improvement using only the provided materials.
- Return the exact change you would make and why it is enough.

Output contract:
- Produce one concise final artifact with body under 1,200 characters.
- State which evidence you inspected.
- State the implementation or design recommendation.
- State open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why.

finalArtifact.title: Add per-arm minimum board-row expectations to the task packet
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected: only this task packet (Experiment goal, Current harness shape, Known measurement risks, Task, Output contract).

Recommendation: extend the packet with a short 'Per-arm board-row expectations' section that names the minimum durable rows each arm must publish so the scorer can compare arms on board evidence alone:
- single: coordination.final only.
- orchestrator: >=1 coordination.work row (assignment) + coordination.final.
- choreography: >=1 coordination.claims + >=1 coordination.findings (or coordination.reviews) + coordination.final.

Why it is enough: the packet already names the third measurement risk ('choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not by hidden driver state') but the Output contract only mandates coordination.final. That asymmetry lets an orchestrator or choreography arm pass scoring with the same artifact a solo arm produces, erasing the very signal the experiment exists to measure. A few lines pinned to the contract close the gap without changing the harness or channels.

Open questions:
- Should reviews be required for choreography or only when disagreement arises?
- Should the orchestrator arm publish a coordination.questions row when delegating, to make 'central coordination' visible vs a single work row?
- Is a row-count threshold (>=1) enough, or should the scorer also require distinct claimantSessionId values to distinguish real peer choreography from a solo agent emitting peer-shaped rows?

finalArtifact.title: Add explicit tool-boundary preamble to task packet
finalArtifact.body:
Recommendation: Add a one-paragraph 'Tool boundary' preamble at the TOP of every scenario task packet (before 'Provided materials'), naming the four forbidden tool families explicitly: shell, filesystem/terminal, repository inspection, and Firegrid execute. State 'Use only Firegrid session_new/session_prompt/wait_for and channel send/wait_for'. This directly mitigates Known Measurement Risk #1 (agents burning time on unavailable tools). It is enough because the packet already lists channels and the final-artifact contract; the only missing piece is an up-front negative scope. Evidence inspected: the task packet text itself (sections 'Known measurement risks', 'Task', 'Output contract') and the 'Important tool boundary' note that already appears mid-packet — promoting it to the top is a pure relocation, no new policy.

Child sessions created: (A) doc-gap worker — recommended hoisting the tool-boundary note above the substrate description so it is read before an agent starts hunting, naming forbidden tool families explicitly; (B) workflow-gap worker — assignment dispatched (run-incomplete-without-coordination.final mechanism), reply not yet observed at publish time. The final adopts A's framing because it is a pure documentation change requiring no driver/scorer code.

Open questions: Should the scorer also emit a structured 'cause' field when coordination.final is missing (Worker B's lane)? Should the preamble be templated into the scenario generator so all arms inherit it automatically?

Coordination claim: Delegation produced one concrete redirect (promote existing mid-packet boundary note to the top) that I would not have framed as 'pure relocation, zero new policy' without Worker A's phrasing.

finalArtifact.title: Require coordination.final body to cite the board rowIds it converged from
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected (task packet only):
- Driver scores a run by waiting for coordination.final; run is incomplete without it.
- Rules already gate final on >=1 claim AND >=1 finding/review present.
- Stated risks: optional-prose finals make cross-arm scoring unreliable; judging should be durable-board-based, not hidden driver state.
- Board state this run: 1 claim (planner), 1 finding (planner) -- no peer rows arrived within 60s on solo-baseline.

Recommendation:
Amend the participant prompt's coordination.final output contract with one bullet: 'body MUST cite the board rowIds (or {channel, workId, claimantSessionId}) of the claim and finding/review you converged from; do not claim coordination helped unless those rows exist.'

Why this is enough:
- Keeps final as the single scored artifact -- no harness/schema change.
- Forces a durable provenance trail on the board, so reviewers can mechanically distinguish choreography (multiple distinct claimants cited) from solo-baseline (only own rows cited) without consulting driver state.
- Solves both named risks at once: prose becomes structured-enough to score, and scoring stays on durable rows.

Coordination evidence for THIS run: I did NOT observe peer rows -- only my own planner claim + finding. Citing only own rows here, consistent with solo-baseline; no claim that coordination helped.

Open questions:
- Promote citations to a typed field (citedRowIds: string[]) on coordination.final as a follow-up?
- For solo-baseline, is self-citation sufficient or should the contract require it explicitly?

scenario.name: Review And Revision
scenario.hypothesis: A draft-review-revise task should reveal whether extra participants improve correctness enough to justify overhead.
scenario.expectedDivergence: central or choreography should show explicit critique/revision evidence; single may be faster but more likely to miss edge cases.
scenario.taskPacket:
# Agent Coordination Patterns Task Packet

This packet is self-contained. Do not inspect the repository, shell, or filesystem.
Use only the Firegrid session and coordination-channel tools exposed to you.

Scenario: Review And Revision

Provided materials:

Experiment goal:
Firegrid should act as the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: one participant, a central orchestrator, and peer choreography.

Current harness shape:
- The driver composes a real Firegrid host in-process.
- Participants are launched through the public Firegrid client/session surface.
- The shared board is exposed as channels: coordination.work, coordination.claims, coordination.findings, coordination.questions, coordination.reviews, and coordination.final.
- The driver injects inbound events through Firegrid.channels.send and waits for the final artifact through Firegrid.channels.waitFor.
- A run is incomplete unless a coordination.final row is published.

Known measurement risks:
- If the task packet asks for repository or shell access, agents may burn time on unavailable tools instead of exercising Firegrid coordination.
- If final artifacts are optional prose instead of channel rows, the scorer cannot reliably compare arms.
- Choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not by hidden driver state.

Task:
- Draft a small improvement plan for the experiment harness.
- Review the plan for failure modes or measurement bias.
- Revise the plan once based on the review.
- The final artifact must separate draft, review findings, and revised plan.

Output contract:
- Produce one concise final artifact with body under 1,200 characters.
- State which evidence you inspected.
- State the implementation or design recommendation.
- State open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why.

finalArtifact.title: Review-and-revision: harness improvement plan (single arm)
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected: only the task packet (no repo/shell access available or used).

DRAFT PLAN:
1. Make coordination.final the sole success oracle; scorer ignores prose.
2. Add board-schema validation at send time (kind, title, status, workId).
3. Emit per-arm trace of channel rows with monotonic seq for replay.
4. Provide a packet-only contract; forbid repo/shell tool calls in arms.
5. For choreography arm, score on durable board state (claims/findings/reviews/final) not driver memory.

REVIEW FINDINGS:
A. Bias: single-arm has no claims/reviews; scoring across arms must not penalize empty boards when the protocol does not require them.
B. Failure mode: schema validation at send hides arm bugs; should warn+record, not drop.
C. Measurement risk: per-arm trace seq may diverge if host clock used; need logical seq from channel itself.
D. Hidden state: 'durable board behavior' still depends on what the driver waitFor matches on; match predicates must be published with the run.
E. Tool-boundary leak: packet says no shell, but agents may still attempt; need explicit deny + observable refusal metric.

REVISED PLAN:
1. Scorer keyed on coordination.final only; per-arm rubric declares which other channels are required (single: none; orchestrator: work+claims+final; choreography: findings+reviews+final).
2. Validate final payload shape on receive; surface schema misses as a scored defect, do not drop.
3. Use channel-assigned sequence for traces; publish driver waitFor predicates alongside the run id.
4. Record tool-boundary violations (shell/fs attempts) as a separate metric, not a hard fail.
5. Keep packet self-contained; include the rubric the scorer will apply.

RECOMMENDATION: Adopt revised plan; land rubric+predicates as part of the run manifest so arms are comparable.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- Is logical channel seq already monotonic across arms?
- Should refusal-to-use-shell be rewarded or just neutral?
- How is partial progress credited when an arm publishes final but skips intermediate channels its rubric required?

finalArtifact.title: Review-and-Revision: Harness improvement plan (central arm)
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected: task packet + replies from two child sessions (drafter, reviewer) via session.agent_output TextChunks.

DRAFT (from drafter child): plan bullets included contract-enforced coordination.final schema/scorer; agents told only Firegrid tools (no shell/fs); pre-flight self-check rejecting prose-only finals; structured per-arm trace of claims/findings/reviews counts for scoring parity.

REVIEW (from reviewer child): top biases to guard: arm-asymmetric instrumentation; tool-access drift (must hold identical channel/tool/permission surface per arm); prose-vs-row final ambiguity; hidden driver coupling leaking choreography state; scoring leakage via tool-allowlist diffs.

REVISED PLAN: (1) Make coordination.final a typed row with required fields {kind,title,body,status,workId,arm}; reject prose-only. (2) Fix identical tool surface per arm (only Firegrid session+channel tools; no shell/fs/exec). (3) Driver observes ONLY channel rows; assert no out-of-band reads. (4) Per-arm counters (claims/findings/reviews/questions) emitted as a meta row. (5) Pre-flight harness self-check that exercises each channel once before the timed run.

Recommendation: ship (1)+(2)+(3) as the minimum viable parity fix; (4)+(5) follow.

Open questions: how to score choreography fairness when peers stay silent; whether to bound child-session count per arm; how to detect a participant that fabricates rows it never acted on.

Coordination effect: delegation produced both a draft and an independent bias checklist before revision; the reviewer's tool-access-drift point reshaped bullet (2) from 'document tools' to 'enforce identical surface'.

finalArtifact.title: Final: Experiment harness improvement plan (review-revision)
finalArtifact.body:
DRAFT: Board-only oracle; driver schema-validates; require claims+(findings|reviews) before final; <=1200-char final; arm-agnostic probe each peer >=1 non-final row; synth questions row for missing tools.

REVIEW: F1 oracle needs deterministic fold. F2 driver-as-judge contaminates arm; schema must be peer-consumed contract. F3 defer-vs-discard unspecified. F4 driver speaking for peers contaminates choreography. B1 board createdAt only. B2 uniqueness metric. B3 arm-blind scoring. B4 truncation = board row. E1 final tiebreak; E2 cycle bound; E3 claim TTL; E4 empty-board timeout; E5 per-runId scoping; E6 per-section budgets.

REVISED: R1 fold by (createdAt,rowId), dedup keep-last. R2 schemaVersion on rows; peers self-reject; driver never judges. R3 defer not discard; latency=acceptedAt-firstWork. R4 truncation receipt = findings row. R5 probe scored unlabeled. R6 driver only publishes capability manifest. R7 board timestamps only. R8 unique/total per channel. R9 final tiebreak min createdAt then rowId. R10 cycle cap=3 stalemate. R11 claim TTL=120s. R12 empty-board 180s timeout. R13 channel-layer runId scoping. R14 sections <=350/350/400. R15 only structured finals=complete. Coord helped: reviewer F2/F4 reframed R2/R6; E1-E6 produced R9-R14 absent from draft.

scenario.name: Webhook Burst Triage
scenario.hypothesis: Bursty inbound events should reveal whether arms can deduplicate, prioritize, and avoid over-coordinating under load.
scenario.expectedDivergence: single may process linearly; central should delegate independent incidents; choreography should show durable claims preventing duplicate work.
scenario.taskPacket:
# Agent Coordination Patterns Task Packet

This packet is self-contained. Do not inspect the repository, shell, or filesystem.
Use only the Firegrid session and coordination-channel tools exposed to you.

Scenario: Webhook Burst Triage

Task:
- Watch coordination.work for inbound webhook incidents.
- Wait for these inbound workIds on coordination.work before finalizing: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-102:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, github:check:failed.
- Use scalar matches such as match.workId for those waits; do not guess payload timestamps.
- Group duplicate incidents by external entity.
- Triage severity and publish findings.
- The final artifact must list the deduped incident groups and which evidence rows supported each group.

Output contract:
- Produce one concise final artifact with body under 1,200 characters.
- State which evidence you inspected.
- State the implementation or design recommendation.
- State open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why.

inboundSignals.title: linear.issue.updated TF-101
inboundSignals.body: Issue TF-101 changed labels from bug to regression.

inboundSignals.title: linear.issue.updated TF-102
inboundSignals.body: Issue TF-102 received a customer escalation comment.

inboundSignals.title: linear.issue.updated TF-101 duplicate
inboundSignals.body: Duplicate delivery for TF-101 with the same external event id.

inboundSignals.title: github.check.failed
inboundSignals.body: CI failed for a related branch; failure signature mentions unknown-channel.

finalArtifact.title: Webhook Burst Triage — deduped incident groups
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected (4 coordination.work rows):
- linear:TF-101:a — linear.issue.updated TF-101 (labels bug→regression, atMs 0)
- linear:TF-101:duplicate — duplicate delivery, same external entity TF-101 (atMs 500)
- linear:TF-102:a — linear.issue.updated TF-102, customer escalation comment (atMs 250)
- github:check:failed — CI failure on related branch, signature mentions unknown-channel (atMs 1500)

Deduped incident groups by external entity:
1) linear/TF-101 (sev: high — regression label) — rows: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate (duplicate suppressed by workId/external id).
2) linear/TF-102 (sev: high — customer escalation) — rows: linear:TF-102:a.
3) github/check (sev: medium — CI red, unknown-channel signature; likely correlates to TF-101 regression but no explicit link) — rows: github:check:failed.

Recommendation: dedupe webhooks by (source, externalEntityId, externalEventId) at ingest; bucket downstream triage per entity. Severity ladder: customer escalation ≥ regression-label > CI-red. Treat unknown-channel CI failure as a correlation hint, not a merge key.

Open questions: is the duplicate redelivery from provider retry or a replay-storm bug? Should github/check link to TF-101 via branch metadata? Confirm dedupe window (workId-stable vs time-bounded).

Coordination value: single-arm — coordination board only used to receive inputs via scalar workId waits; no multi-agent claim/finding traffic was needed.

finalArtifact.title: Webhook burst triage — central orchestrator
finalArtifact.body:
Deduped incident groups (by external entity):

1) linear:TF-101 — severity P2 (label flip bug→regression). Evidence rows: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate (same external event id, deduped).
2) linear:TF-102 — severity P1 (customer escalation comment). Evidence rows: linear:TF-102:a.
3) github:check:failed — severity P1 (CI failure; signature mentions 'unknown-channel'). Evidence rows: github:check:failed. Correlation hint: 'unknown-channel' signature is a known channel-routing failure class but no direct linkage to TF-101/TF-102 from the evidence given.

Evidence inspected: 4 coordination.work rows above + child analyst replies.

Children created: Analyst-A (Linear lane) confirmed TF-101 dedup pair + TF-102 singleton; Analyst-B (GitHub lane) flagged check:failed with unknown-channel hint. Their replies shaped the dedup grouping and severities.

Recommendation: dedup webhook handlers by (source, external entity id) before triage; route P1 customer-escalation + CI failures to oncall; treat regression-label flips as P2 review.

Open questions: Is the 'unknown-channel' CI failure linked to either Linear ticket? Need a correlator over branch→ticket. Did orchestration help here? Marginally — only 1 of 4 work rows had ambiguity needing a specialist; lane split added overhead vs payoff.

finalArtifact.title: Webhook burst triage — deduped groups + recommendation
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected (coordination.work rowIds):
- 520c320d linear:TF-101:a — TF-101 label bug→regression
- 60be49da linear:TF-101:duplicate — same external id as TF-101:a
- 80d09943 linear:TF-102:a — TF-102 customer escalation
- c8458570 github:check:failed — CI failed, signature mentions 'unknown-channel'

Deduped groups:
G1 Linear TF-101 regression — rows [520c320d, 60be49da]. Suppress :duplicate; canonical=TF-101:a. Severity: medium.
G2 Linear TF-102 escalation — rows [80d09943]. Severity: high (customer escalation).
G3 GitHub CI failure — rows [c8458570]. Severity: medium.

Recommendation: dedup by external-entity id (linear issue key) + delivery id at the webhook ingestor; do not merge G3 into G1 on source proximity alone — require a signature match. Triage order: G2 → G1 → G3.

Open questions:
1. Does the github 'unknown-channel' signature reference the same code path as TF-101's regression? Need a confirming evidence row before merging G3 into G1.
2. Is the duplicate delivery a webhook-provider retry or an at-least-once replay from our edge? Affects whether dedup belongs at ingestor or downstream.

Coordination value: peers (planner/builder/reviewer) are spawned and may publish their own claim/finding/review/final rows on the board; this final reflects the conductor-peer slice only — additional peer artifacts are expected on the channels.
